title: Serpocaulon vacillans

More specifically, it is categorized as a member of the genus Serpocaulon, which is a group of ferns native to the Americas. The classification of Serpocaulon vacillans is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the division Pteridophyta. This division includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores.

intro: Serpocaulon vacillans, also known as 'Vacillating Rattan', is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Serpocaulon vacillans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Serpocaulon vacillans include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions.
